description: Demo of reinforcements & escalation (Tactical Crucible #33), authored entirely from the existing trigger system — no special engine code. Two bandits hold a watch-post; the moment you drop the first, the survivor sounds the alarm and a fresh pair charges in from the gate. The fight has momentum and a clock: cut them down fast, or silence the caller, before the second wave lands. Verify: kill a bandit and watch a wave spawn (once), with a log announcing it.
customIntroduction: A pair of road-bandits keep a wary watch over the waystation gate — and a battered iron bell hangs from the lintel beside them. The moment blades are drawn, one of them edges toward the bell-rope.
customContext: DEMO — REINFORCEMENTS (#33). This is a COMBAT scene showing escalation authored from existing triggers (`npc_killed` → `spawn_enemy_at`), not new engine code. When the first bandit falls, the survivor 'rings the bell' and a second wave of two bandits charges in from the gate (the `bandit_alarm` trigger, fires `once`). PROCEDURE: narrate the alarm and the new arrivals when the wave trigger fires (you'll see them appear + the DEMO log line); play the tension of a timer — the longer the fight drags, the more arrive. Combine naturally with morale (#34, already in the engine): once the player turns the tide, wounded bandits will flee and the last may surrender. The point: a fight with momentum and a clock, where speed and silencing the caller matter — built from data, so any encounter can author waves on a kill, an HP threshold, or a turn.
objective: Hold the gate. Drop the bandits fast — the first to fall rings for help, and a second wave charges in. Win before the reinforcements bury you.

then.message: DEMO (#33): reinforcements. When the first bandit falls, a wave charges in from the gate. Drop them fast — or silence the caller — before more arrive. (Authored from existing triggers; pairs with engine morale #34: survivors will flee or surrender once routed.)
The bell clangs — reinforcements charge in from the gate!
